source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Diamond Ocean Blackhawk

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Synthetic Based Mud Discharge
actions_taken: Packer pressure checked once per hour with manual adjustment of lower pressure limit as needed
Packer management Low Limit Set Points monitored hourly in addition to normal maintenance checks
Operations put on hold until operator created and implemented prevention plan
Approved plan established with BSEE before operations resumed
event.summary: While performing drilling operations on the Diamond Ocean Blackhawk for Anadarko Petroleum Corporation, two incidents occurred in which Synthetic Based Mud was inadvertently discharged into the Gulf of Mexico. The first incident on July 4, 2015 released 2.5 barrels of SBM, and the second on August 8, 2015 released 3.3 barrels of SBM. Both incidents were caused by a malfunction in the rig's software for the Slip Joint Packer management system, combined with inadequate crew monitoring of packer pressures. The software logic automatically adjusted low alarm set points based on instrument air pressure fluctuations without crew knowledge, causing the packer system to vent mud when pressure dropped below the adjusted set point.
recommendations: Manufacturer to develop and install upgrade to packer management system logic to prevent automatic adjustment of low alarm set points without crew knowledge
key_phrases: automatic adjustment of low alarm set points

threats.name: Unintended activation of Slip Joint Packer vent mode due to software logic malfunction
Inadequate monitoring of packer pressure by crew
Automatic adjustment of packer low alarm set points without crew knowledge

consequences.name: Discharge of Synthetic Based Mud to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Slip Joint Packer Management System with Low Pressure Alarm
controls.barrier_role: Automatic detection and response to loss of packer seal pressure
controls.lod_basis: Automated system designed to detect pressure loss and engage secondary packer

supporting_text: The software logic for the packer management system will automatically adjust the low alarm set points based on fluctuations in the instrument air over a period of time
The initial packer set point of 89 psi had increased to 99.1 psi without the crew's knowledge
As the instrument air pressure was dropped from 106 psi to 98.7 psi, the low set point was reached and the system logic took over

controls.name: Crew Monitoring of Packer Pressure
controls.barrier_role: Manual detection and prevention of pressure drop below critical thresholds
controls.lod_basis: Operator procedure requiring crew to monitor packer pressures during operations

supporting_text: Failure of the rig's personnel to adequately monitor the packer pressure to ensure that they were not nearing their lower set points
Failure of the rig's personnel to monitor all systems while they attempted to adjust the Diverter Pilot

controls.name: Diverter Regulator Pressure Control Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unintended pressure changes to packer system during diverter adjustments
controls.lod_basis: Procedure to manage instrument air pressure adjustments to diverter regulator

supporting_text: The crew had decreased the instrument air supply by approximately 7 psi, dropping both the manifold and packer pressures from 106 psi to 98.7 psi
The instrument air supply and the Slip Joint Packer pressure transducers, for both the Upper and Lower Packers, are tied to one common manifold

controls.name: Continuous Packer Energization
controls.barrier_role: Maintain packer seal integrity during operations
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident corrective action to ensure packers remain energized

supporting_text: Both packers will be continuously energized

controls.name: Hourly Packer Pressure Monitoring and Manual Adjustment
controls.barrier_role: Detect and correct pressure deviations before system malfunction occurs
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident corrective action requiring hourly manual checks and adjustments

supporting_text: Packer pressure is checked once per hour and lower pressure limit is manually adjusted as needed
Packer management 'Low Limit Set Points' are being monitored hourly in addition to normal maintenance checks

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
